sentences.base_language: Miguel arrives at the metro station for the first time.
sentences.target_language: Miguel llega a la estación del metro por primera vez.
sentences.grammarNote:
**Llega a la estación** — **llegar a** = to arrive at. **La estación** is feminine (all **-ción** nouns are feminine).
**Del metro** — **del** = **de + el** (mandatory contraction). **El metro** is masculine.
**Por primera vez** = for the first time. Fixed phrase: **primera** agrees with feminine **vez** (time/occasion).

sentences.base_language: He looks at the map and feels a little confused.
sentences.target_language: Mira el mapa y se siente un poco confundido.
sentences.grammarNote:
**El mapa** — masculine despite ending in **-a**. Greek-origin nouns ending in **-ma** are masculine: el mapa, el problema, el sistema, el programa.
**Se siente** — reflexive **sentirse** (to feel). **Confundido** = confused — past participle used as adjective, agreeing with masculine Miguel.
**Un poco** = a little. Modifies adjectives directly: **un poco confundido**, **un poco cansado**.

sentences.base_language: He approaches a woman. 'Excuse me, do you know where I can buy a ticket?'
sentences.target_language: Se acerca a una señora. —Disculpe, ¿sabe dónde puedo comprar un boleto?
sentences.grammarNote:
**Disculpe** = excuse me (formal) — use with adults you don't know.
**Disculpa** = excuse me (informal) — use with friends or people your own age. From **disculpar** (to excuse/forgive).

sentences.base_language: 'Yes, the ticket machines are right over there, next to the entrance.'
sentences.target_language: —Sí, las máquinas de boletos están allí mismo, junto a la entrada.
sentences.grammarNote:
**Allí mismo** = right there (emphatic, precise location). **Allí** = there (specific) · **allá** = over there (vaguer, more distant).
**Junto a** = next to / beside (close physical proximity). Compare: **al lado de** (next to, same level) · **junto a** (beside, close together).
**Las máquinas de boletos** — feminine plural. **La máquina** = the machine (feminine).

sentences.base_language: Miguel buys a ticket and goes to the turnstiles.
sentences.target_language: Miguel compra un boleto y va a los torniquetes.
sentences.grammarNote:
**Va a los torniquetes** — **va a** + plural noun. No contraction with **los** (contractions only happen with **el**: **a + el = al**). **A + los** stays as **a los**.
**Un boleto** = a ticket (Latin America). Spain uses **un billete**.
**Los torniquetes** = the turnstiles (masculine plural).

sentences.base_language: He sees two different lines on the sign. Which one does he need?
sentences.target_language: Ve dos líneas diferentes en el letrero. ¿Cuál necesita él?
sentences.grammarNote:
**Ve** = he sees (from **ver**, irregular yo: veo). Don't confuse with **ve** from **ir** (go) — context distinguishes.
**¿Cuál?** = which one? (from a set). **¿Qué?** = what? (more open-ended). **¿Cuál necesita?** asks him to choose between known options.
**El letrero** = the sign/notice (masculine). Also: **el cartel** (poster/sign), **el anuncio** (announcement/advert).

sentences.base_language: He asks a station worker. 'Which line goes to the historic center?'
sentences.target_language: Le pregunta a un empleado de la estación. —¿Qué línea va al centro histórico?
sentences.grammarNote:
**¿Qué línea va a...?** and **¿Por dónde se va a...?** are both common ways to ask for transit directions.
Note: a + el contracts to **al**: al centro, al aeropuerto, al norte.

sentences.base_language: 'Take Line 2, direction Tasqueña. Get off at Zócalo station.'
sentences.target_language: —Tome la Línea 2, dirección Tasqueña. Bájese en la estación Zócalo.
sentences.grammarNote:
**Bájese** is the formal usted imperative of **bajarse** (to get off). The -se makes it reflexive.
**bajarse** (get off) / **subirse** (get on) — heard constantly on public transit. Informal: bájate, súbete.

**¿Cuántas paradas son?** = How many stops is that? **Cuántas** is feminine plural, agreeing with **paradas** (feminine).
**Son** (from **ser**) — here **ser** is used for quantity/number (a defining characteristic). Compare: **¿Cuántos años tiene?** (How old is he? — ser for age as a number).
**La parada** = the stop (feminine). The same word for bus stop: **la parada de autobús**.

sentences.base_language: 'About six stops. It takes around twelve minutes.'
sentences.target_language: —Unas seis paradas. Se tarda unos doce minutos.
sentences.grammarNote:
**Unas seis paradas** — **unas** = approximately (feminine plural). **Unos/unas** before numbers = about/around.
**Se tarda** = it takes (time). **Tardar** means to take time: **se tarda diez minutos** (it takes ten minutes), **tarde mucho** (I took a long time).
**Unos doce minutos** — **unos** (masculine plural) agreeing with **minutos**.

sentences.base_language: 'Thank you so much. You've been very helpful.'
sentences.grammarNote:
**Ha sido** (has been) is the present perfect tense: haber (conjugated) + past participle. 'Ha sido muy amable' is a polite, slightly formal way to thank someone.
In everyday speech: **¡Muy amable!** on its own works as a shorthand.

sentences.base_language: Miguel gets on the train and finds a seat by the window.
sentences.target_language: Miguel sube al tren y encuentra un asiento junto a la ventana.
sentences.grammarNote:
**Sube al tren** — **subir a** = to get on (transport). **Al** = **a + el** (mandatory contraction — **tren** is masculine).
Compare: **subir al tren** (get on the train) · **bajarse del tren** (get off the train — **del** = **de + el**).
**Encuentra** — stem-changing verb **encontrar** (o→ue): encuentro, encuentras, **encuentra**.
